title: Diego and the Lost Baby Bird
synopsis: When Diego finds a scared baby bird in his backyard, he feels worried but becomes brave to help find the bird's mama, leading to a happy reunion and new friendship.

pages.text: Diego was playing in his backyard when he heard a tiny sound. "Peep! Peep!" He looked around and found a small baby bird sitting alone under the big oak tree. The little bird looked scared and sad.

illustration.description: A young Latino boy with dark curly hair and brown eyes, wearing a bright red t-shirt and blue shorts, crouching down near a tiny yellow and brown baby bird under a large oak tree. Diego's face shows concern and curiosity. The backyard has green grass, colorful flowers, and a wooden fence. Bright, cheerful cartoon style with soft textures.
illustration.alt: Diego discovering a baby bird in his backyard

pages.text: "Oh no! Where is your mama?" Diego asked gently. The baby bird just peeped softly. Diego felt worried. The little bird needed help, but Diego wasn't sure what to do. He wanted to be brave and help his new feathered friend.

illustration.description: Close-up of Diego kneeling next to the baby bird, his face showing worry and determination. The baby bird has big, sad eyes and fluffy yellow feathers with brown spots. Diego is reaching out his hand slowly and carefully. The background shows dappled sunlight through tree leaves, creating a warm, gentle atmosphere with golden and green tones.
illustration.alt: Diego looking worried as he tries to help the scared baby bird

pages.text: Diego looked up at the tall tree branches. "Maybe your mama is up there," he said. He could hear other birds chirping high above. Diego took a deep breath and felt brave. "Don't worry, little bird. I will help you find your family!"

illustration.description: Diego standing up and looking up at the oak tree with his hand shading his eyes, showing a brave and determined expression. The baby bird sits nearby on the grass. The tree stretches up with thick brown branches and green leaves, with small colorful birds visible in the upper branches. Bright blue sky peeks through the leaves with warm sunlight streaming down.
illustration.alt: Diego looking up at the tree, feeling brave and ready to help

pages.text: Diego ran inside to get his mom. "Mama, there's a baby bird that needs help!" His mom, Mrs. Rivera, smiled and followed him outside. She had kind brown eyes and wore a colorful apron. "Let's help together, mijo," she said warmly.

illustration.description: Diego holding his mother's hand as they walk outside together. Mrs. Rivera is a warm Latina woman with long dark hair in a bun, wearing a bright floral apron over a yellow blouse. She has a gentle, caring expression. They're walking through a doorway from their cozy home with terracotta tiles and potted plants by the entrance.
illustration.alt: Diego and his mother coming outside to help the baby bird

pages.text: Mrs. Rivera gently picked up the baby bird and placed it on a low tree branch. Suddenly, a mama bird with beautiful blue and yellow feathers flew down! She chirped happily when she saw her baby. Diego felt so happy and proud that he had been brave and helped.

illustration.description: Mrs. Rivera carefully placing the baby bird on a low branch while Diego watches excitedly. A beautiful mama bird with bright blue and yellow feathers is flying down toward them with wings spread wide and a joyful expression. The baby bird is chirping happily on the branch. The scene is filled with warm golden sunlight and shows the happy reunion moment.
illustration.alt: The joyful moment when mama bird finds her baby with Diego and his mother helping

pages.text: The mama bird sang a beautiful thank-you song to Diego and his mom. Diego waved goodbye as the bird family flew up to their nest together. "We did it, Mama!" Diego said with a big smile. He felt happy and brave, knowing he had helped a friend in need.

illustration.description: Diego and his mother waving goodbye as the mama bird and baby bird fly up toward their nest in the oak tree. Both Diego and Mrs. Rivera have big, happy smiles. The birds are flying together with the baby bird staying close to its mama. The background shows a beautiful sunset with orange and pink colors in the sky, and the tree nest visible among the green leaves.
illustration.alt: Diego and his mother waving goodbye to the reunited bird family

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Diego feel when he first found the baby bird?
questions.explanation: Diego felt worried because the baby bird looked scared and sad, and he wasn't sure how to help at first.

questions.question: Who helped Diego with the baby bird?
questions.explanation: Diego's mom, Mrs. Rivera, came outside to help him with the baby bird.

questions.question: What happened when the mama bird found her baby?
questions.explanation: The mama bird was happy to find her baby and sang a beautiful thank-you song to Diego and his mom.

questions.question: How did Diego feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Diego felt happy and brave because he had helped the baby bird find its mama.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words - Page 2
pages.text: Diego felt _____ and wanted to help. His _____ came outside to help him find the mama bird.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words - Page 3
pages.text: The mama bird sang a _____ song. Diego felt _____ that he had helped his new friend.
